contract.current_promise: Future changes remain backward-compatible with 0.1 or use a new version number; M4A owns supported-version ranges, negotiation, migration, and rejection policy.

current.section_order: constants, names, main, zero or more function sections, optional debug_sources, optional debug_locations
current.parser_policy: Rust parsing is strict: required sections, sequential references, instruction shapes, debug references, and trailing/unknown sections are rejected.
current.formatter_policy: Rust dump canonicalizes a parsed program to the same cdbc 0.1 text representation used by the checked-in reference corpus.
current.debug_policy: Debug metadata is additive and optional; source/function/instruction indexes are artifact-local, while line and column values are one-based.

evolution_decisions.current_state: The cdbc family header, .cdbc extension, compiler emit mode, and Rust dump/run commands identify the current artifact kind.
evolution_decisions.named_consumer: C++ BytecodeTextEmitter and Rust format parser/CLI
evolution_decisions.rationale: No second artifact family is currently transported or consumed.

revisit.condition: Introduce a distinct artifact family only if a concrete consumer cannot use the cdbc 0.1 envelope.

evolution_decisions.current_state: Optional debug_sources and debug_locations sections are emitted, strictly parsed, and used by the Rust VM for source-aware runtime diagnostics.
evolution_decisions.named_consumer: Rust VM runtime diagnostics and debugger-oriented M4B work
evolution_decisions.rationale: The existing additive metadata already covers the current source-mapped runtime consumer without changing core execution sections.

revisit.condition: Extend metadata only when a named debugger/runtime consumer requires a field not expressible by the current sparse mappings.

evolution_decisions.current_state: The Rust parser is strict and rejects an unexpected section instead of silently skipping it.
evolution_decisions.rationale: Strict rejection protects the current canonical parser/formatter contract and prevents an older VM from silently misinterpreting new semantics.

revisit.condition: Change only with an explicit versioned forward-compatibility policy and parser tests for unknown sections.

evolution_decisions.current_state: The artifact does not declare a target triple, VM identity, or runtime feature set; the current compiler and Rust VM are the only named consumer pair.
evolution_decisions.named_consumer: M4A compatibility validator, if multi-runtime or cross-target transport is introduced
evolution_decisions.rationale: No current transport or consumer needs to select among runtimes or targets, so adding an unconsumed field would create speculative ABI surface.

revisit.condition: A second runtime, target, or artifact transport requires deterministic compatibility rejection or selection.

evolution_decisions.current_state: The current version has one fixed opcode/native capability set and no negotiation handshake.
evolution_decisions.rationale: Capability bits without a consumer-defined negotiation or rejection rule would be metadata without semantics.

revisit.condition: A compatible extension needs feature discovery while preserving a supported 0.1 reader/writer path.

evolution_decisions.current_state: Current .cdbc output is one linked program artifact; module-interface text is inspection metadata and not a separate-compilation input.
evolution_decisions.named_consumer: M3A/M3B module graph and separate/incremental compilation
evolution_decisions.rationale: Module identity belongs to the module graph and separate-compilation decision; it is not needed in the current whole-program artifact.

revisit.milestone: M3A/M3B, then M4A if serialized module products use cdbc
revisit.condition: Choose whether modules link into one final artifact or carry independently validated artifact/interface identities.

evolution_decisions.current_state: The contract is a line-oriented UTF-8 file; no streaming, concatenation, embedded transport, or binary consumer exists.
evolution_decisions.rationale: A new framing layer would add format and implementation cost without a concrete transport requirement.

revisit.condition: A named transport requires bounded records, random access, binary encoding, or corruption-resilient framing.

evolution_decisions.current_state: The current artifact has no embedded digest or signature; local tests compare complete text files and the Rust parser validates structure.
evolution_decisions.named_consumer: M4A artifact transport/cache or security validator, if introduced
evolution_decisions.rationale: Structural parsing and repository golden comparison cover the current local workflow; an integrity field needs a defined trust boundary and failure policy.

revisit.condition: Artifacts cross an untrusted transport or are cached/shared where authenticity or corruption detection is a stated requirement.

evolution_decisions.current_state: No successor version or negotiation range is selected; the parser accepts exactly cdbc 0.1 and rejects other family/version headers before execution.
evolution_decisions.named_consumer: M4A compatibility matrix, migration/rejection policy, and version-aware Rust parser
evolution_decisions.rationale: A breaking artifact change would need a new version, but M0.5B finds no current requirement that justifies selecting one now.

revisit.condition: A selected artifact/runtime change cannot remain a compatible extension of 0.1 and has a concrete producer/consumer migration plan.

successor_decision.decision: No cdbc successor is required or selected at M0.5B.
successor_decision.rationale: The current 0.1 envelope already provides the emitted core sections, strict Rust parse/dump, optional debug metadata, and the only current compiler/VM consumer pair. All proposed additions without a concrete consumer or transport are deferred or not currently required. M4A remains authorized to select a successor if a later compatibility requirement proves that a 0.1-compatible extension is insufficient.
triggers: separate compiled module artifacts requiring a new envelope
a named transport/security requirement that cannot fit a compatible extension

invalid_input_policy.reference_corpus: tests/malformed_cases.json cdbc mutations plus vm-rs format unit tests
